---
name: Feature request
about: Propose a change to Codexa's behavior or scope
labels: ["feature"]
---

<!--
Before filing, please skim CONTRIBUTING.md, specifically the "Scope is
deliberately narrow" section. Many requests — new language indexers, new
LLM-based layers, general-purpose search modes — will be closed as
out-of-scope even when they are clearly useful somewhere.

An issue-first conversation saves everyone time. Do not send a PR for
anything beyond a typo fix without an issue thread first.
-->

## What you want

<!-- One paragraph. What should Codexa do that it doesn't today? -->

## Why — the problem

<!--
What are you trying to accomplish? If this is a workaround for a different
limitation, say so; the real fix might be elsewhere.
-->

## What you tried

<!--
Did you try an existing verb, a different flag, a raw-search call, etc.?
If so, what did it return, and why wasn't that enough?
-->

## Scope check

<!--
Honest answers make this easier to triage:

  - Does this require a new dependency?
  - Does this require a configuration system to toggle?
  - Does this widen Codexa's language coverage?
  - Does this introduce an LLM call from within Codexa?

If any are yes, it is more likely to be closed as out-of-scope. If you think
the value justifies the scope expansion, say why.
-->
